player_utterance: Quiet night for something like this.

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, paper cup of cold coffee in one hand, looking out past the candle into the dusk

player_utterance: Some people need the room. Some people need the door.

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, shifts his weight, looks down at his untied shoe and doesn't bend to fix it
standing very still, the paper cup held a little tighter, eyes on the player

player_utterance: What shape does it take from the inside?

believer_state_after.posture: moves to the bench, sits down slowly, sets the cold coffee on the small table beside the candle
sitting on the bench, hands on knees, not looking at the player, watching the candle flame lean

voice_offers.read: He sat down. That is an answer. Let it breathe.
He's seated, softened — name the thing he won't.
voice_offers.line: You haven't said his name once tonight.

player_utterance: What does it say about him?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, funeral program open on his knee, reading it sidelong in the candlelight

voice_offers.read: Silence held twice; a third would slacken, not deepen.
voice_offers.line: Say his name, John. Once. Out loud.

voice_offers.read: He's watching the flame; let the question be small.
voice_offers.line: Was he the one who taught you to watch a candle like that?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, program flat on his knee, hands going still

voice_offers.read: He hides in the program; force him out of it.
voice_offers.line: Put the program down, John. I asked you a question.

voice_offers.read: He's reading it for a reason; meet him there.

player_utterance: Your hands stopped moving when you got there.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, hands still on the program, aware now of being watched

voice_offers.read: He hides behind scripture; strip the costume off.
voice_offers.line: That's not an answer. That's a verse. Do you believe it, or are you hiding behind it?

voice_offers.read: His hands went still — meet that, don't break it.

player_utterance: You don't have to say it perfectly. Just... say it.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, both hands flat on the program now, head bent

voice_offers.read: He noticed the noticing; press the seam open now.
voice_offers.line: Whose name is on that program that you can't say?

voice_offers.read: He is caught; let him find his own words.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to quote anything to me, John.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, the program slides off his knee onto the bench beside him, hands open on his thighs now, palms up

voice_offers.read: He is already breaking; pressure now would be cruelty.
He's bent; one true word from him ends this.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, hands still open on his thighs, looking at the candle now instead of the player

voice_offers.read: His hands opened. The silence is doing the work now.
Palms up is surrender. One name now and it's done.

player_utterance: What do you think he meant?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, eyes still on the candle, one hand lifts to rub the back of his neck and falls again

voice_offers.read: He's collapsing inward; presence matters more than words now.
Silence has worked twice; one true question lands now.

player_utterance: Whatever he meant — your grandfather already put the Word in your hands. It's still there.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, head bowed, one hand pressed flat against his chest pocket where no book is

voice_offers.read: He's broken open; the question is already inside him.
voice_offers.line: Don't tell me what he meant. Tell me what you couldn't say to him.

voice_offers.read: His hand reached for his own neck. Meet him there.
voice_offers.line: Whatever he meant — you don't have to answer me. Just breathe.

player_utterance: Your hand knows where it used to live.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, looking down at his open hand resting on his thigh, fingers slightly curled as if around something not there

voice_offers.read: He's bowed, hand on empty pocket; let the image hold.
He's breaking — name what his hand is reaching for.
voice_offers.line: Your hand keeps going to that pocket. What did you used to carry there, John?

player_utterance: Whose hand taught yours to hold it?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, hand resting open on his thigh, looking out past the candle into the dark of the courtyard

voice_offers.read: He's collapsing quietly; pushing now would be cruelty.
voice_offers.line: Whose hand did you used to hold, John?

voice_offers.read: He's almost there; name what's missing from the hand.
voice_offers.line: Say his name, John. Out loud. Once.

believer_state_after.posture: stands slowly from the bench, picks up the cold coffee cup, looks down at the program left on the bench, picks it up too, folds it once more, slides it into his inside jacket pocket

voice_offers.read: He is already there; let the question land.
His hand is open — put the name in it.
voice_offers.line: Say it. The name of the man who taught you.
